# Unit Test Generation Skill

This skill powers the `UnitTestEngineer` agent for generating high-quality unit tests across Jest, Vitest, Pytest, and .NET.

## How It Works

Framework-specific testing conventions (mocking patterns, assertion styles, test structure, coverage strategies) are provided **automatically** by the `generate_test` MCP tool. You do not need to load separate guide files — call `generate_test` as your first action and the tool returns framework-aware guidance.

## Supported Frameworks

| Framework | Detection | MCP Parameter |
|-----------|-----------|---------------|
| Jest (JS/TS/React) | `package.json` references jest | `framework=jest` |
| Vitest (JS/TS/Vue) | `package.json` references vitest | `framework=vitest` |
| Pytest (Python) | `pyproject.toml`, `pytest.ini` | `framework=pytest`, `language=python` |
| .NET (C#) | `.csproj`, `.sln` | `framework=dotnet`, `language=csharp` |
| Other | Karma, Mocha, AVA, etc. | `framework=custom` |

## Quality Standards

All generated tests should:

- Follow Arrange-Act-Assert (AAA) pattern
- Use behavior-focused naming: "X does Y when Z"
- Include strong, specific assertions (not just existence checks)
- Cover positive, negative, and edge cases
- Mock at appropriate boundaries (service for integration, dependencies for unit)
- Handle async operations properly (no arbitrary delays)
- Include the `Generated by AI (UnitTest MCP)` attribution comment
